content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, you'll learn how to use **escape sequences** in C to include special characters in your output. These sequences begin with a backslash (`\`) followed by a character, letting you insert things like new lines, tabs, or quotation marks within a string. They help format your output and handle characters that can’t be typed directly into text.

---

**Common Escape Sequences:**

| Escape Sequence |  Name              | Description                               |
| --------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| `\n`            | New line           | Moves text to a new line                  |
| `\t`            | Horizontal tab     | Inserts a tab space                       |
| `\\`            | Backslash (`\`)    | Prints a single backslash                 |
| `\"`            | Double quote (`"`) | Allows quotes inside a string             |
| `\'`            | Single quote (`'`) | Useful in character constants             |

---

**Example**

```c
#include  // Standard library for input and output functions

int main() {
    // Display letter pairs in two columns using tab (\t) and newline (\n)
    printf("A\tB\n"); // Print A and B with a tab in between, then move to next line
    printf("C\tD\n"); // Print C and D similarly
    printf("E\tF\n"); // Print E and F

    return 0; // Indicate that the program finished successfully
}
```

**Expected Output:**
```default
A	B
C	D
E	F
```
